title: Neural networks of the mouse visceromotor cortex
methods: anterograde and retrograde neuroanatomical tract tracing (AAV, PHAL, BDA, CTB, Fluorogold)
RNAscope in situ hybridization for cell-type-specific gene expression
MORF3 sparse labelling with light-sheet and confocal 3D morphology reconstruction
channelrhodopsin-assisted circuit mapping (CRACM) with single-cell patch-clamp
miniscope calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) during social and non-social behaviour
optogenetic activation with plasma corticosterone assay
data_sources: Allen Brain Atlas (ABA) in situ hybridization database
Outspector custom annotation software (UCLA Dong lab)

data_scope.tracer_injections_main: DPs n=4 anterograde/n=3 retrograde; DPd n=2/n=1; ILA, PL, ACAd, ACAv n=3 each
data_scope.cross_validation_injections: >96 additional tracer experiments targeting cortex, hippocampus, amygdala, thalamus, hypothalamus, brainstem
data_scope.calcium_imaging_dp: 6 male VGLUT2-Cre mice, average 63.3 ± 9.9 neurons per animal
data_scope.calcium_imaging_ila: 6 male mice, average 133.5 ± 14.4 ILA neurons per animal
data_scope.cracm_crh: 12 out of 14 CRH neurons responded to DPd optogenetic stimulation
data_scope.data_access: open code and annotated cases at github.com/ucla-brain/mpf; interactive viewer at brain.neurobio.ucla.edu/mpf

core_conclusion: The dorsal peduncular area (DP) of the mouse medial prefrontal cortex comprises deep (DPd) and superficial (DPs) sublayers that form the core visceromotor cortex, directly projecting to hypothalamic and brainstem structures to govern neuroendocrine, sympathetic and parasympathetic outputs. The DP functions as a rare unidirectional cortical hub, receiving convergent lateral-cortical inputs and relaying them forward to medial cortical areas with minimal reciprocal feedback. Notably, DPd neurons directly innervate hypothalamic CRH neurons, and their optogenetic activation acutely elevates plasma corticosterone. A unified MPF network model is proposed integrating these findings.
why_it_matters: Resolves definitional ambiguity of the DP across brain atlases; discovers a direct cortical-to-neuroendocrine pathway bypassing assumed indirect relays; identifies a topologically unique unidirectional hub; provides an open platform resource (interactive viewer + annotated connectome data + code); advances mechanistic understanding of stress, anxiety, depression and social-behaviour circuits.
why_nature: Landmark-scope unified MPF wiring diagram synthesizing neuroanatomy, physiology and behaviour; overturns textbook assumptions about cortical governance of stress hormones; exceptional methodological breadth (tracing + optogenetics + CRACM + calcium imaging); high translational relevance to stress disorders and psychiatric disease.
added_value_to_my_research: Connectome-constrained network modelling = structural-prior graph learning applicable to spatial-network GNNs with road/admin constraints
Graph-theory hub metrics (betweenness centrality, modularity) for brain circuits directly transfer to spatial service-hub identification in facility-location optimization
Neural foundation model with wiring-diagram inductive bias = geospatial foundation model with spatial-graph structural prior for transfer across cities
Compact interpretable models with architectural structural priors = explainable ML where geographic adjacency is encoded as model structure rather than post-hoc
Unidirectional hub topology as design principle for asymmetric multi-source data fusion architectures in GeoAI

related.relation: foundation model of neural population activity generalizing across stimulus contexts — extension of calcium-imaging results

related.title: Compact deep neural network models of the visual cortex

related.relation: brainwide coordinated network dynamics and initialization principles relevant to MPF network modelling

figures.caption: Anatomic delineation and cell-type organization of the DP — cytoarchitecture, connectivity-based parcellation, molecular markers, dendritic morphology of 47 reconstructed neurons
Cortical input/output organization of DPs, DPd and other MPF areas — retrograde tracing maps, hierarchical clustering, unidirectional information flow schematic
DP projections to autonomic-output structures — DPs→PVHd/LHA sympathetic pathway, DPs→CEA→DMX parasympathetic disynaptic route, Barrington's nucleus projections
DPd projects directly to hypothalamic neuroendocrine zone — PVH CRH neuron innervation confirmed by CRACM (12/14 neurons responded); optogenetic activation elevates plasma corticosterone
Functional role of DP neurons — multimodal sensory convergence (ENTl, PIR, AUDv); calcium imaging showing preferential activation during social vs non-social interactions
Proposed unified MPF network model — DPs/DPd/ILA as visceral motor cortex; DP as hub of unidirectional cortical information flow; whole-brain 3D wiring schematic
